symptom: Users encounter unexpected account type selection page (Work or school account vs Personal account) during every sign-in, affecting both public cloud and Mooncake accounts, including managed and federated accounts.
rootCause: MSA (Microsoft Account / LiveID) service throttling. Customer public IP sends >200K requests/day to MSA service (threshold: 200K/IP/24h). EvoSTS in every cloud (public, Mooncake, Fairfax) queries MSA to check if username is MSA. Throttling in one cloud affects all clouds. When throttled, isOtherIdpSupported flag returns incorrect value causing the account type selection page to appear.
solution: Check MSA throttling status via ESTS Kusto PerRequestTableIfx across all clusters (estsam2/estsbl2/estswus2/estsch1/estsdb3/estssin/estsdm1/estssn1), filter by ClientIp, check MsaExists==Throttled. Customer must split outbound requests across multiple public IPs, or identify which internal project generates excessive requests. MSA service cannot whitelist IPs.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Authentication (EvoSTS)/Case study/2207270040003645 - unexpected account type selecti.md

symptom: Need to verify JWT token signature issued by Azure AD/Entra ID to confirm token authenticity. jwt.io shows signature as not valid but that does not necessarily mean token is invalid.
rootCause: jwt.io requires the correct signing certificate to validate. Azure AD signing certificates are published at the OpenID well-known endpoint. For Mooncake: https://login.partner.microsoftonline.cn/<tenantid>/v2.0/.well-known/openid-configuration -> jwks_uri -> x5c value.
solution: Option 1: Use jwt.io with correct cert. Option 2: PowerShell - get x5c from https://login.partner.microsoftonline.cn/<tenantid>/discovery/v2.0/keys, save to .cer file, then use RSA signature verification script (SHA256Managed + X509Certificate2.PublicKey.Key.VerifyHash). Note: managed identity tokens also use the same signing cert endpoint.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Authentication (EvoSTS)/Case study/How to verify JWT signature.md

symptom: Users report that KMSI (Keep Me Signed In) sessions do not last the expected 90 days. Users are re-prompted for credentials unexpectedly even with KMSI enabled.
rootCause: Multiple factors affect KMSI session duration beyond the 90-day refresh token max inactivity: (1) Non-persistent session tokens expire in 24h, persistent ones in 90d. (2) Password change/reset revokes all password-based refresh tokens. (3) Conditional Access policies may enforce MFA or re-auth. (4) IP/location/device changes trigger security checks. (5) Browser cache/cookie clearing deletes session cookies. (6) Incognito mode prevents cookie persistence. (7) Intune device compliance policies.
solution: Session cookie tracks authentication state (not tokens), refresh token stored separately. When session cookie expires but refresh token is valid, silent re-auth occurs. To adjust session retention: use CA Session controls (Sign-in Frequency). For Azure Portal: configure Signing out setting. Reference: learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/conditional-access/concept-conditional-access-session
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Authentication (EvoSTS)/Case study/Questions on Session Cookies and Refresh Tokens fo.md

symptom: Need to understand when Azure AD refresh tokens and session cookies are revoked vs stay alive across different credential change scenarios.
rootCause: Token revocation behavior depends on token type and trigger: Password-based cookies/tokens are revoked on password change, SSPR, or admin reset. Non-password-based cookies/tokens survive password changes. User/admin revoking refresh tokens revokes ALL types. Single sign-out only revokes cookies, not tokens.
solution: Revocation matrix: (1) Password expires -> all stay alive. (2) User changes password -> password-based cookie+token revoked, non-password-based stay. (3) SSPR -> same as password change. (4) Admin resets password -> same as password change. (5) User revokes tokens -> ALL revoked. (6) Admin revokes tokens -> ALL revoked. (7) Single sign-out -> cookies revoked, tokens stay. Reference: learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity-platform/refresh-tokens#token-revocation
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Authentication (EvoSTS)/Case study/Questions on Session Cookies and Refresh Tokens fo.md

symptom: Connect-AzAccount in Azure Automation fails with error: Federated service returned error ID3242: The security token could not be authenticated or authorized. User is a managed account but auth flow goes to ADFS federated endpoint.
rootCause: Another user object exists with ProxyAddresses containing the same SMTP address (e.g., smtp:admin@domain), causing user realm discovery to match the wrong user object. The wrong object is federated, so MSAL sends credential to ADFS instead of AAD managed auth endpoint. ADFS rejects with ID3242 because the user does not exist there.
solution: Debug with $debugPreference=Continue before Connect-AzAccount to see MSAL logs. Check user realm discovery response. In ASC, search UPN and look for duplicate user objects with overlapping ProxyAddresses. Delete or fix the conflicting user object with wrong ProxyAddresses. Also try other users to confirm it is user-specific.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Authentication (EvoSTS)/Case study/User realm discovery mismatch.md

symptom: SSO not working on AADJ (Azure AD Joined) device when accessing on-prem file share via CNAME hostname (e.g., \\fs01.child.contoso.com which is CNAME to fs1.contoso.com). Manual credential input works. Same access works on Hybrid Joined devices.
rootCause: Two-layer failure: (1) Kerberos fails because no SPN is registered for the CNAME hostname (KRB5KDC_ERR_S_PRINCIPAL_UNKNOWN). (2) NTLM fallback fails on AADJ devices because they send AzureAD\samaccountname as domain prefix instead of the correct AD domain\samaccountname. Hybrid Joined devices correctly send child\samaccountname for NTLM. AADJ devices lack on-prem domain context.
solution: Fix Kerberos: register SPNs for the CNAME hostname: setspn -S host/fs01.child.contoso.com fs1 and setspn -S host/fs01 fs1. Fix NTLM: sync DN (distinguishedName) attribute of users to cloud so AADJ devices get proper domain\samaccountname for NTLM auth. Either fix alone can resolve the issue. Verify with network trace comparing TGS-REQ/NTLMSSP_AUTH User field.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Authentication (EvoSTS)/Case study/[AAD]SSO not working on AADJ device when access on.md

symptom: Need to understand ESTS RamLogData entries in sign-in logs to troubleshoot why a sign-in was blocked or flagged as risky by Azure AD Identity Protection.
rootCause: RAM (Risk Assessment Module) is the ESTS runtime engine that evaluates all online protection algorithms. It produces 3 aggregated recommendations: overall, account risk (for CA account risk policies), and session risk (for CA session risk policies). Key recommenders: SmartLockout (blocks after bad password threshold), IPBlocking (dynamic IP blocking based on bad/good ratio), Tor (flags anonymous network IPs), FamiliarFeatures (unfamiliar location detection), OfflineAccountRisk (MACE/ML pipeline risk), GeoHopping (globe-jumping MFA bypass detection).
solution: Read RamLogData in sign-in logs. Key fields: RecommendedAction (0=NotSet, 1=DoNotDisturb, 40-90=Risk levels, 100=Block), RecommenderId (1=Lockout, 2=IPBlocking, 4=Tor, 5=FamiliarFeatures, 6=OfflineAccountRisk, 7=GeoHopping, 9=IPBlockingOverride), RecommenderExecutionStatus (10=Available, 20=Degraded, 30=Unavailable), AuthenticationStatus (1=Success, 2=PreviousPassword, 3=BadCredential, 4=RepeatedBadPassword). Complex algorithm-specific issues should be escalated to Protection on-calls.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Authentication (EvoSTS)/RamLogDataDetails.md

symptom: Customer security scan flags Azure AD ESTS login cookies (wlidperf, ESTSAUTHLIGHT, brcap, ch, clrc) on login.partner.microsoftonline.cn as non-HTTPOnly, raising security concern.
rootCause: These cookies are set by client-side JavaScript for non-security purposes: brcap detects touch capabilities (value 0 or 1), clrc is a client resource cache hash. They are not involved in AuthN/AuthZ and contain no sensitive data.
solution: Explain to customer: 1) These cookies are created by client-side JS, not the server, so HTTPOnly flag cannot be set (JS cannot set HTTPOnly on cookies it creates). 2) Cookie contents are non-sensitive (touch capability flag, resource cache hashes). 3) They do not affect authentication/authorization. Reference: IcM-337767815.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Authentication (EvoSTS)/Session cookies non-HTTPOnly.md

symptom: Need to assign Microsoft Graph API application permissions (app roles) to a managed identity in Azure China. Azure portal does not provide UI for this.
rootCause: Azure portal lacks UI to assign Graph API app roles to managed identities. Must be done programmatically via PowerShell.
solution: Use Microsoft.Graph PowerShell module: 1) Connect-MgGraph with appropriate scopes (Application.ReadWrite.All, AppRoleAssignment.ReadWrite.All). 2) Get-MgServicePrincipal to find target app and its AppRoles. 3) Filter AppRoles by Value (e.g., Application.Read.All). 4) New-MgServicePrincipalAppRoleAssignment with -ServicePrincipalId (target app SP), -PrincipalId (managed identity object ID), -ResourceId (target app SP), -AppRoleId (role ID).
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Dev/MS Graph API/Known issue/Grant graph api permission to managed identity.md

rootCause: Microsoft Graph PowerShell SDK does not have a pre-registered first-party application in Azure China (unlike public Azure), so interactive login without specifying -ClientId fails.
solution: Register application manually in Azure China portal following MS Learn guide (Build PowerShell scripts with Microsoft Graph). Then connect with: Connect-MgGraph -Environment China -ClientId <registered-app-id> -UseDeviceAuthentication -TenantId <tenant-id>.

rootCause: Authentication methods resources were not deployed to Azure China Graph API. Now partially available in beta version.
solution: (Legacy workaround - now beta API available) Option 1: Use Azure portal UI (AAD > Users > Authentication methods) for individual users. Option 2: For bulk operations, use internal AAD UEX API - retrieve bearer token from portal network trace (TenantSkuInfo request), then PATCH https://main.iam.ad.ext.azure.cn/api/UpdateUser/<objectId>/MfaProperties with body containing authenticationPhoneNumber, authenticationEmail. symptom: Az PowerShell Connect-AzAccount succeeds but subsequent cmdlets (Get-AzVM) fail with SharedTokenCacheCredential authentication unavailable Multiple accounts matching error. Clear-AzContext does not help.
rootCause: Multiple stale account entries in MSAL shared token cache file (%LOCALAPPDATA%\.IdentityService\msal.cache). When multiple accounts with same UPN+tenantId exist in cache, SharedTokenCacheCredential cannot disambiguate.
solution: Delete msal.cache from %LOCALAPPDATA%\.IdentityService\. Also delete ~/.azure/AzureRmContext.json and AzureRmContextSettings.json. Use procmon to trace which cache files PowerShell reads during login to identify stale caches.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Dev/MSAL/Clear Az PowerShell MSAL token cache.md

symptom: Authentication requests to Entra ID fail with AADSTS1002016 error, HTTP 400 response, UnsupportedTls message. Affects HTTP clients using TLS 1.0/1.1 protocols.
rootCause: Entra ID deprecated TLS 1.0, TLS 1.1 and 3DES cipher suites. Mooncake started incremental blocking from Dec 2023 in 6 phases over 4-5 weeks. Only tenanted requests blocked initially for exception grant capability.
solution: Enable TLS 1.2 on client OS/apps per MS Learn guide. Check current TLS with testtls.com. Use Kusto (estscnn2) PerRequestTableIfx where MaskedResponse contains 1002016. For temporary exception contact eSTS team. Portal: portal.azure.cn Manage Legacy TLS blade.

symptom: Hybrid Azure AD joined device user cannot obtain PRT. 3rd-party federation service (VMware IDM) sits between ADFS and AAD in chain: Browser -> ADFS -> VIDM -> AAD.
rootCause: During PRT acquisition Windows sends WS-Trust active auth request to federation active endpoint. VMware IDM returns 401 Unauthorized because it does not support WS-Trust active auth endpoint required for PRT. ADFS never receives the request.
solution: Capture Fiddler trace during user lock/re-login. Compare good (ADFS-direct) vs bad (VIDM) traces at step 4 (active auth). Good flow: UserRealm discovery -> federation metadata -> nonce -> active auth SAML -> PRT exchange. Fix: bypass VIDM for PRT flow.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Device Registration/MC Case Study/2211070040001618 unable to get PRT due to 3rd-part.md

symptom: SAML 2.0 IDP federation between AAD and SAP IAS+IDS fails during token validation. Login works with IAS alone (no IDS chaining). AAD rejects the SAML response.
rootCause: When IAS chains with IDS (IDP proxy), SAML response includes AuthenticatingAuthority with value accounts.sap.com which is relative URI. Per SAML spec 1.3.2 all URI values MUST be absolute (RFC 2396). AAD ReadSimpleUriElement rejects non-absolute URIs.
solution: Configure IDS entityID to use absolute URI (e.g. https://accounts.sap.com). SAP later modified their URL. Note: AAD does not support AuthnContextClassRef PreviousSession which may cause additional failures.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/SAML IdP federation/Case study/119060326000336 SAML 2.0 IDP Federation between AA.md

symptom: SAML federation between AAD and SAP IAS+IDS fails with AADSTS500132 Assertion is malformed. Works fine with IAS alone without IDS.
rootCause: When IAS integrates with IDS, NameID format changes from SAML 2.0 persistent to SAML 1.1 unspecified. AAD SAML 2.0 federation does not accept SAML 1.1 NameID format. IAS docs confirm it forwards IDS NameID format directly to SP.
solution: Configure IDS to return SAML 2.0 format NameID. Or modify IAS metadata to only include SAML 2.0 NameID formats. User provisioning must set ImmutableID matching NameID value.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/SAML IdP federation/Case study/119060326000336 SAML 2.0 IDP Federation between AA.md

symptom: User can login PowerBI via desktop/browser but mobile app fails with AADSTS50126 Error validating credentials. Sign-in logs show one success and one failure for mobile login.
rootCause: User email is in federated domain but actual UPN is in managed domain. Mobile app broker (Authenticator) handles domain routing differently from desktop, routing password validation to managed domain endpoint instead of federated IDP.
solution: Check ASC for user UPN vs email domain type (federated vs managed). Collect Authenticator logs from mobile. Compare mobile vs desktop auth flows. Align user UPN with federated domain or ensure correct federation routing.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/SAML IdP federation/Case study/2208240040000739 AAD Unable to login Mobile PowerB.md
Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/SAML IdP federation/Case study/SAML 2.0 IDP federation wrong config, causing stra.md
symptom: SAML 2.0 IDP federation with OKTA in 21V causes strange login experience. User reaches Office portal instead of expected destination. AAD interacts with OKTA via WS-Fed instead of SAML protocol.
rootCause: Domain federation protocol in MSODS was configured as WS-Fed instead of SAML. OKTA only supports SAML (sending RelayState), but AAD expected WS-Fed (wctx parameter). Protocol mismatch caused broken authentication flow.
solution: Reconfigure the domain federation to use SAML protocol: 1) Export existing federation settings via Get-MsolDomainFederationSettings. 2) Set-MsolDomainAuthentication -Authentication Managed to revert to managed. 3) Re-federate with -PreferredAuthenticationProtocol Samlp parameter. 4) Verify with Get-MsolDomainFederationSettings.

sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/SAML IdP federation/Case study/[FAQ] Common issue.md
symptom: Customer wants to configure 3rd-party SAML 2.0 IdP federation with Azure AD (21V) for SSO to O365 but unsure about required attributes and setup process.
rootCause: SAML 2.0 IdP federation requires 3 mandatory attributes in the IdP token: (1) Issuer - IdP URI, (2) NameID - ImmutableID (Source Anchor from AAD Connect, Base64 encoded), (3) IDPEmail - User Principal Name (UPN). Missing or incorrect attributes will fail authentication.
solution: Configure the SAML 2.0 IdP federation via PowerShell (Set-MsolDomainAuthentication). Ensure IdP returns all 3 required attributes. Reference: https://docs.azure.cn/en-us/active-directory/hybrid/how-to-connect-fed-saml-idp. Note: 3rd-party IdP configuration is outside Microsoft support scope.

sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/SAML IdP federation/Case study/[FAQ] Common issue.md
symptom: SAML 2.0 IdP federation returns HTTP 400 error after sending SAML AuthnRequest. No SAML response received from IdP.
rootCause: Customer confused the sign-on URL and sign-out URL when configuring the SAML IdP federation. The URLs were swapped in the federation configuration.
solution: Verify and correct the PassiveLogOnUri (sign-on) and LogOffUri (sign-out) in the federation configuration. Ensure they match the IdP metadata.

sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/SAML IdP federation/Case study/[FAQ] Common issue.md
symptom: SAML assertion validation fails with error code 500081: SAML assertion validation failed: no supported token signature is provided.
rootCause: The 3rd-party IdP did not enable signature on the SAML assertion/response. Azure AD requires the SAML assertion to be signed with a supported algorithm.
solution: Enable SAML assertion signing on the IdP side. Ensure the signing certificate is correctly configured and the signature algorithm is supported by Azure AD (typically RSA-SHA256).

sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Sync/AAD Connect/Case study/[Sync issue] The object with the attribute "msExch.md
symptom: AAD Connect sync error: The object with the attribute msExchRecipientTypeDetails = 2 could not be synced to the cloud.
rootCause: msExchRecipientTypeDetails = 2 indicates Linked Mailbox recipient type. Azure AD Connect has a known limitation that does not support syncing users with Linked Mailbox.
solution: This is a by-design limitation. Users with Linked Mailbox (msExchRecipientTypeDetails=2) cannot be synced to Azure AD. Workaround: change the mailbox type if business requirements allow.

sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/EXTENSION/## TSG & Case Sharing/Wrong join type cause AdDomainExtension failure.md
symptom: Set-AzVMADDomainExtension fails with error: Exception(s) occurred while joining Workgroup and errCode 1332. Computer account creation fails (error 0x534).
rootCause: JoinOption 0x00000001 (NetSetupJoinDomain) only performs domain join without creating the computer account in AD. If the computer account does not pre-exist, the join fails with error 1332/0x534.
solution: Use JoinOption 0x00000003 (NetSetupJoinDomain + NetSetupAcctCreate) to both join and create the account. Or pre-create the computer account. Check logs at C:\WindowsAzure\Logs\Plugins\Microsoft.Compute.JsonADDomainExtension and C:\Windows\debug\netsetup.log.

sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune 2/## Feature Update/MC721851：Update your PowerShell scripts with a Mic.md
symptom: Intune PowerShell scripts using the global Microsoft Intune PowerShell application ID (d1ddf0e4-d672-4dae-b554-9d5bdfd93547) stop working after April 2024 deprecation.
rootCause: Microsoft deprecated the global Intune PowerShell application (client) ID as part of updated authentication methods in the Graph SDK-based PowerShell module.
solution: Create a new app registration in Microsoft Entra admin center. Update all scripts to use new app ID. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/entra/identity-platform/quickstart-register-app. Internal KB: IT781707.

sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune 2/## Android TSG/when you enable Device Based Access for SharePoint.md
symptom: Android users cannot access SharePoint Online or browser-based Exchange Online after enabling device-based Conditional Access despite device being enrolled in Intune.
rootCause: On Android, device-based CA for browser access requires Enable Browser Access option in Company Portal/Authenticator. Without it, browser cannot pass device compliance info. Edge requires Work/School sign-in; Chrome requires certificate install.
solution: Instruct user: 1) Open Company Portal or Authenticator. 2) Settings > Enable Browser Access. 3) Chrome: sign out of O365, restart, accept cert. Edge: sign in Work/School account.

symptom: GPO-based automatic MDM enrollment fails on Windows device. Task Scheduler task for MDM enrollment is not created. GPresult shows MDM Policy extension skipped with ReadGPOList count = 0.
rootCause: A local Group Policy setting was enabled that blocks all local GPO processing. With local GPO processing blocked, the MDM enrollment policy (set via local GPO) cannot be applied, and the Task Scheduler task for automatic MDM enrollment is never created. Additionally, if MDM authority is not set to Intune, enrollment will still fail even after the GPO issue is resolved.
solution: 1) Check if any setting is blocking local GPO processing. Look for ReadGPOList count = 0 in GPSVC logs. 2) Deploy the Enable automatic MDM enrollment using default Azure AD credentials policy via Domain GPO instead of local GPO. 3) Verify MDM authority is set to Intune (not Unknown) in Intune portal. 4) After fixing, verify Task Scheduler task is created and device enrolls successfully. Use dsregcmd /status to verify AAD PRT.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune 2/## Mooncake Case Study/20870760 - GPO not create Task Scheduler with Enab.md

symptom: iOS Teams app unable to login. Teams is blocked by Conditional Access policy requiring compliant application (MAM). Teams shows MAM token acquisition failure while other apps (Outlook, OneDrive, Word) work normally on the same device.
rootCause: Teams app retains a stale/old OID in its Intune MAM upnCache after user account changes (e.g., intern leaving and re-joining as full-time creates new OID). Teams keeps using the old OID for MAM token requests while Outlook correctly uses the new OID. The old OID no longer exists in Azure AD, so MAM token acquisition fails. Root cause is Teams using an older Intune SDK version that does not properly refresh the OID cache.
solution: 1) Check IntuneMAM logs for upnCache entries - if two different OIDs map to the same identity (UPN), the app is using a stale OID. 2) Compare Intune SDK versions between Teams and Outlook. 3) Uninstall and reinstall Teams may temporarily fix. 4) File IcM requesting Teams PG to update their Intune SDK version. 5) Long-term fix requires Teams to ship with updated Intune MAM SDK.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune 2/## Mooncake Case Study/2510280030001846 - Teams unable to login.md

symptom: iOS Teams app fails to login with error, blocked by CA policy. ESTS logs show ErrorCode 53005 (ProtectionPolicyRequired) and 700082 (ExpiredOrRevokedGrantInactiveToken) for Teams while other MAM-protected apps work fine.
rootCause: Teams app has stale MAM enrollment data with an expired/revoked refresh token. The Teams upnCache contains two OIDs for the same UPN - one old and one new. Teams attempts to use the old OID for MAM token acquisition, gets auth error, then falls back to interactive auth which also fails because the refresh token is expired (700082). CA then blocks with 53005 because Teams is not MAM-protected.
solution: 1) Analyze IntuneMAM logs: look for two OIDs in upnCache for same identity. 2) Check ESTS PerRequestTableIfx for ErrorCode 700082 and 53005. 3) Kusto: cluster(estscnn2).database(ESTS).PerRequestTableIfx where TenantId and CorrelationId to trace auth flow. 4) Have user completely uninstall Teams, clear all data, reinstall. 5) If issue persists due to SDK version, escalate to Teams PG via IcM.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune 2/## Mooncake Case Study/[To be organized]2506250030000735 IDO VW Teams.md

symptom: Windows OOBE Autopilot enrollment succeeds despite CA policy enforcing device compliance for all resources. CA report shows failure but device is enrolled and becomes compliant after policies deploy.
rootCause: By design, Microsoft Intune Enrollment is not blocked by CA policies requiring device compliance, even when the policy scopes to all cloud apps without exclusions. The enrollment process uses a special flow that bypasses the compliance check during initial enrollment. After enrollment completes, compliance policies deploy and device becomes compliant. The CA failure report during enrollment is expected.
solution: 1) It is NOT necessary to exclude Microsoft Intune Enrollment from CA policies requiring device compliance. 2) The CA failure report during OOBE enrollment is expected behavior. 3) After enrollment completes, verify compliance policies are deployed and device becomes compliant. 4) The enrollment flow uses DSReg for device registration (TPM-bound key pair) followed by MDM enrollment, both complete before CA compliance is enforced.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune 2/## Windows TSG/Windows Enrollment/Windows OOBE enrollment process with CAP.md

symptom: WHfB PIN prompt appears during sign-in even though WHfB is set to Disabled in Intune tenant-wide Windows Enrollment policy. Identity Protection profile is configured to enable WHfB for specific user groups but the disable takes priority.
rootCause: Intune tenant-wide WHfB policy (Windows Enrollment) and Identity Protection profile use different registry paths: tenant-wide writes to Device config (HKLM PassportForWork TenantId Device Policies UsePassportForWork=0), while Identity Protection writes to User config (per-SID path). The tenant-wide Disable setting takes absolute priority - Identity Protection Enable cannot override it.
solution: 1) To selectively enable WHfB for specific groups while keeping it off for others: Set tenant-wide WHfB policy to Not Configured (not Disabled). 2) Use Identity Protection profiles assigned to specific groups to enable WHfB. 3) Alternatively, use OMA-URI custom profile with ./User/Vendor/MSFT/PassportForWork/TenantId/Policies/UsePassportForWork=True. 4) Note: Changing tenant-wide from Disabled to Not Configured only affects NEW device enrollments, not existing devices.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune 2/## Windows TSG/Windows Hello for Business (WHFB_WH4B).md

symptom: AADJ device users prompted to setup PIN during OOBE despite no explicit WHfB policy configured. Customer wants to understand when WHfB PIN setup is triggered during device enrollment.
rootCause: If no WHfB policy is configured (tenant-wide = Not Configured), Windows provisions WHfB by default during AAD sign-in. This is built-in behavior. The Identity Protection profile for user groups takes effect slightly later than the default WHfB provisioning during AAD sign-in.
solution: 1) To prevent PIN prompt during OOBE: Set tenant-wide WHfB to Disabled. 2) To allow PIN during OOBE with defaults: Leave tenant-wide as Not Configured. 3) To customize: Set tenant-wide to Enabled with desired settings. 4) Configuration Profiles take effect AFTER enrollment, so for OOBE-time control, use the tenant-wide setting. 5) After enrollment, Configuration Profiles override defaults on next workspace account sign-in after profile syncs.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune 2/## Windows TSG/Windows Hello for Business (WHFB_WH4B).md

symptom: Users can access SharePoint site via web browser but cannot download/print/sync content. Error: Your organization does not allow you to download, print, or sync using this device.
rootCause: SharePoint admin configured unmanaged devices access to Allow limited, web-only access. This auto-creates 2 CA policies: one blocks apps from non-HAADJ/non-Intune devices, another restricts browser to read-only.
solution: Either join device to Azure AD (HAADJ) or enroll in Intune. Or have SharePoint admin adjust the unmanaged device access setting. CA policies auto-created by SharePoint.

symptom: CA policy with Require app protection policy grant control fails for Edge on Windows. MAM enrollment not recognized, sign-in blocked with AADSTS53005 or suberror protection_policy_required.
rootCause: Edge MAM flags not enabled or enrollment ID mismatch. Edge sends x-ms-enrollments header with base64 encoded enrollment ID to AAD. If mismatch with EnrollmentIds in CA policy, access blocked.
solution: Verify MAM in Edge via edge://edge-dlp-internals. Requires Windows 11 22H2+, Edge v115+, Intune license, Entra ID P1. Decode x-ms-enrollments in Fiddler. Device must be Workplace Joined not MDM enrolled.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune 2/## Windows TSG/App protection policy for Edge on Windows.md

symptom: User blocked from resources in Chrome/Firefox when CA has Require app protection policy. Redirected to Launch in Edge.
rootCause: Non-MAM-aware browsers do not support MAM enrollment. CA with Require app protection policy requires MAM-enabled browser. Expected behavior.
solution: Switch to MAM-enabled Edge. If CA has both Require app protection AND Require compliance device with OR, non-MAM browsers prompt MDM extension instead. Route to Edge support SAP.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune 2/## Windows TSG/App protection policy for Edge on Windows.md

symptom: Device completed AADJ during OOBE but user not in MDM auto-enrollment group. After adding user to group, device does not auto-enroll. Manual enrollment creates personal-owned device.
rootCause: Once AADJ done during OOBE without auto-enrollment, opportunity missed. UI enrollment creates MDMFull type 0 (personal), not MDMDeviceWithAAD type 6 (corporate).
solution: Use Group Policy to trigger auto-enrollment post-AADJ. GP creates MDMDeviceWithAAD type 6 (corporate). Ref: learn.microsoft.com enrollment using group policy.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune 2/## Mooncake Case Study/2403290030002442 _ Enroll after AADJ.md

symptom: Hybrid AADJ Autopilot provisioning delayed 3-4 hours. ESP stuck at Joining your organizations network. Device registration much slower than expected.
rootCause: Automatic-Device-Join scheduled task disabled after domain join. Stays disabled 1-2 hours. First registration attempt after enabling fails with 0x801c03f3 (device not found in AAD).
solution: Force-enable task via Intune PowerShell during White Glove: Enable-ScheduledTask -TaskPath \Microsoft\Windows\Workplace Join -TaskName Automatic-Device-Join. Allow AAD Connect sync interval.

symptom: Microsoft Intune Enrollment app missing from Azure AD MDM config. Cannot configure MDM auto-enrollment scope. Service principal AppId d4ebce55-015a-49b5-a083-c84d1797ae8c not present.
rootCause: Microsoft Intune Enrollment service principal accidentally deleted from tenant Azure AD.
solution: Delete corrupt entry from Azure portal AAD Mobility. Then PowerShell: Connect-AzureAD; New-AzureADServicePrincipal -AppId d4ebce55-015a-49b5-a083-c84d1797ae8c. Verify: Get-AzureADServicePrincipal | Where DisplayName -like *Intune*.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune 2/## Windows TSG/How to add back Microsoft Intune enrollment.md

symptom: Intune config policy apply delayed 48h after Autopilot v2 on Mooncake. Filter deviceTrustType=AAD join shows Unknown. Policies via filter mode fail to apply immediately.
rootCause: Entra ID no Strong Consistency on national clouds (Mooncake/FairFax). Intune sync from Entra after device creation gets NotFound, falls back to daily retry with 24h delay.
solution: Hotfix deployed 2025-10-30/31. Workaround: user-based groups with filter mode. IcM: Incident-31000000456803.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune 2/## Mooncake Case Study/2509190030003863 _ Configuration Policy apply dela.md

symptom: Cannot restrict local logon on Intune-managed Windows to AAD groups via AllowLocalLogOn CSP. AAD group SID in AllowLocalLogOn not evaluated.
rootCause: Windows limitation: AllowLocalLogOn evaluates SIDs at logon but AAD group SIDs not recognized by Windows security subsystem directly.
solution: Two-step: (1) AllowLocalLogOn with built-in group SID e.g. Remote Desktop Users S-1-5-32-555. (2) LocalUsersAndGroups CSP adds AAD group SID to built-in group. Warning: cannot revert by unassigning, must fix via gpedit.msc.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune 2/## Mooncake Case Study/ee2308170030001393 _ How to add User Rights - Allo.md
Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune 2/## Windows TSG/Windows LAPS with Azure AD/Kusto Query - pending cluster confirmation.md
symptom: Windows LAPS client fails to update local admin password to Azure AD. Event ID 10030 logged in LAPS/Operational event log. Need service-side troubleshooting.
rootCause: Various server-side ADRS issues: tenant discovery failures, device registration errors, password update rejected by Azure AD service.
solution: From Event ID 10030 extract timestamp and correlationId (client-request-id in URL). Kusto: cluster idsharedwus, database ADRS. Query AdrsTraceEvent where correlationId in (ids) and env_time around timestamp. Or use FindTraceLogs(t, delta, ids) function.

sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune 2/## Windows TSG/Windows LAPS with Azure AD/Support Boundaries for Windows LAPS with Azure AD.md
symptom: Unclear which team owns Windows LAPS with Azure AD issues. Case routing confusion between Intune, Windows DS, and Azure Identity teams.
rootCause: Windows LAPS has split ownership: Intune owns MDM policy config/delivery; Windows DS owns LAPS client functionality/crashes/passwords; Azure Identity owns tenant discovery, password retrieval from Azure AD, Graph API, PowerShell cmdlets, audit logs.
solution: SAP routing: MDM policy delivery issues to Intune. LAPS client create/update password failure to Windows DS (Security > Windows LAPS > Manage clients). Tenant discovery or ADRS errors to Identity (Azure AD Directories > Devices > Windows Local Administrator Policy). Password retrieval from portal/Graph/PS to Identity.

sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune 2/## Windows TSG/Windows LAPS with Azure AD/TSG_ How to confirm Intune policy applied to targe.md
symptom: Need to verify whether Intune LAPS MDM policy is actually applied on AADJ/HAADJ Windows client device.
rootCause: Policy delivery may fail due to licensing, enrollment, MDM sync issues. If both GPO and MDM configure LAPS, MDM (Intune) wins by design.
solution: Method 1: dsregcmd /status to verify license + managed state. Then Settings > Accounts > Access work or school > Info > MDMDiagReport.html. Method 2: SyncMLViewer tool shows raw MDM XML, search for LAPS CSP.

sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune 2/## Windows TSG/Windows LAPS with Azure AD/Troubleshooting Windows LAPS with Azure AD -Event.md
symptom: Windows LAPS Event ID 10025 - Azure discovery failed with error 0x80072EE7 (name not resolved). Client cannot reach enterpriseregistration.windows.net.
rootCause: Network connectivity issue: proxy, firewall, or network filtering device blocking access to Azure AD Device Registration Service endpoint.
solution: Verify connectivity: curl https://enterpriseregistration.windows.net/ -D - from admin cmd. If fails, check proxy/firewall rules. Also try Reset-LapsPassword -Verbose -Debug.

sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune 2/## Windows TSG/Windows LAPS with Azure AD/Troubleshooting Windows LAPS with Azure AD -Event.md
symptom: Windows LAPS Event ID 10059 - Azure returned HTTP 403: authorization_error during password update.
rootCause: Device lacks authorization to update LAPS password in Azure AD tenant. Possible tenant-level LAPS policy restriction or device registration issue.
solution: Check LAPS policy in Azure AD tenant. Verify device registration (dsregcmd /status). Check ASC Tenant Explorer > Audit Logs (Service: Device Registration Service, Category: Device).

sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune 2/## Windows TSG/Windows LAPS with Azure AD/Troubleshooting Windows LAPS with Azure AD -Event.md
symptom: Windows LAPS Event ID 10059 - Azure returned HTTP 400: error_missing_device during password update.
rootCause: Device object missing from Azure AD. Deleted, never registered, or stale object purged.
solution: Re-register device to Azure AD. Verify device in portal > Devices. Check dsregcmd /status. For HAADJ verify AAD Connect sync. Key events: 10029 (success), 10030 (tenant discovery), 10032 (auth failure).

sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune 2/## Windows TSG/Windows LAPS with Azure AD/Troubleshooting Windows LAPS with Azure AD -Event.md
symptom: Windows LAPS Event ID 10058 PasswordAgeDays warning when backup to Azure AD.
rootCause: By design: Azure AD enforces minimum PasswordAgeDays of 7. When value < 7 is set with Azure AD backup, system defaults to 7 and logs warning.
solution: Expected behavior. Set PasswordAgeDays >= 7 for Azure AD backup. Event 10058 is informational. Event 10022 shows full applied policy.

sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune 2/Windows logs/Gather Wireshark trace for Windows enrollment & sy.md
symptom: Windows device MDM enrollment or sync fails. Need network-level diagnostics.
rootCause: Various: proxy/firewall interference, TLS issues, AAD token broker failures, MDM push failures.
solution: (1) Connect to mobile hotspot (cellular). (2) ETL traces via logman for AAD/DeviceManagement/TokenBroker/Schannel. (3) Wireshark capture. (4) Trigger sync. (5) Stop captures. (6) Run IntuneODCStandAlone.ps1.

sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune 2/Windows logs/PRT Log.md
symptom: Hybrid Azure AD Join troubleshooting - PRT not issued, device registration incomplete.
rootCause: Various: certificate issues, DRS connectivity, Kerberos failures, stale device objects.
solution: Collect PRT logs: Download Auth.zip from CSS-Windows/WindowsDiag GitHub. Rename .txt to .cmd. Run start-auth.cmd as admin. Reproduce: lock, wait 5s, unlock+signin. Run stop-auth.cmd. Upload authlogs folder.

sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune 2/Windows logs/TPM.md
symptom: TPM attestation fails during device enrollment or Autopilot. TpmTool.exe shows Ready For Attestation: False.
rootCause: Missing or invalid EK certificate in TPM. TPM maintenance task not completing. EKCertStore registry empty.
solution: Diagnostics: get-tpmendorsementkeyinfo, TpmTool.exe getdeviceinformation, check registry EKCertStore, TpmDiagnostics ekinfo/ekchain, certreq -enrollaik to request AIK, collect Procmon + TPM ETL logs.

sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/M365 Identity IDO/## Common operation_TSG/O365 HRD Cache Cleanup _ Override Guide.md
symptom: After migrating a domain from Gallatin (21V) to Global or vice versa, Entra ID reflects changes in minutes but Office 365 clients cannot login for 72+ hours. O365 HRD cache at odc.officeapps.live.com returns old cloud environment.
rootCause: O365 Home Realm Discovery relies on odc.officeapps.live.com federationProvider endpoint and msoid.<domain> DNS CNAME. These caches are independent from Entra ID and take 72+ hours to refresh after domain migration between clouds.
solution: 1. Verify msoid DNS CNAME updated for new cloud. 2. CSS runs internal HRD cache reset script hitting discovery.svc.cloud.microsoft across 23+ DCs with forceRefresh=true. Internal-only, not for customers. 3. Verify via odc.officeapps.live.com domain resolves to correct cloud.

sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/M365 Identity IDO/## Common operation_TSG/O365 HRD Cache Cleanup _ Override Guide.md
symptom: Root domain in Gallatin with msoid CNAME, sub-domain in Global. Office clients on sub-domain redirected to 21V login instead of Global. Clearing HRD cache does not fix because sub-domain inherits root domain msoid config.
rootCause: Sub-domains inherit HRD config from root domain msoid DNS record. When root domain is in Gallatin with msoid, all sub-domains treated as Gallatin by O365 HRD, even if sub-domain is in a different cloud.
solution: Contact PG with sub-domain name and Global tenant ID. PG uses ECS backend to override sub-domain HRD. Takes ~10 min. Future domain changes need PG again. Unaffected: root in WW + sub in 21V with msoid on sub-domain.

sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======7. Tools========/7.10 [MCVKB][Jarvis] How to map jarvis logs for Az.md
symptom: Need to search Jarvis logs for Identity services in Mooncake (AAD Connect object changes, ESTS auth) but cannot find correct Jarvis namespace. ASC for AD Connect not working in Mooncake.
rootCause: Each Azure service maps to a specific MDS namespace in Jarvis. Without knowing the namespace, cannot locate verbose/trace logs. Mapping stored in ServiceTree metadata.
solution: Query ServiceTree Kusto: cluster(servicetreemooncake.chinanorth3.kusto.chinacloudapi.cn).database(Shared).DataStudio_ServiceTree_ServiceMetadata_Snapshot, filter Type=MDSAccount, join Hierarchy_Snapshot, filter OrganizationName=Identity. Example: AAD Change Tracking = AADCTMCPROD namespace. Query ifxverboseevents/traceevents with objectId/tenantId. Use internalCorrelationId for full chains.

sourceRef: MCVKB/wiki_migration/======VM&SCIM======/23.1 Office product, service plan and licensing.md
symptom: Need to look up M365 product SKU IDs, service plan IDs, and offers for Gallatin/Mooncake. Standard Global references do not apply. Troubleshooting licensing issues like GBL failures or service plan conflicts.
rootCause: Product SKUs, service plans, offers differ between Global and Gallatin. Display name to ID mapping not easily accessible. Since 2024/09/09 license assignment moved to M365 admin center or API only.
solution: Lookup: (1) Graph API: /v1.0/users/{id}/licenseDetails or /v1.0/subscribedSkus. (2) Office portal: portal.partner.microsoftonline.cn/admin/api/users/{objectId}/assignedproducts. (3) Entra portal: main.iam.ad.ext.azure.cn/api/AccountSkus?backfillTenants=true. (4) Internal: M365 Ingestion Analytics Power BI, Catalog Account=Office 365 Sanya for Gallatin. Do NOT share IDs with customers.

sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Dev/MS Graph API/Tool/Jarvis.md
symptom: Need to troubleshoot MS Graph API call failures in Entra ID (e.g. permission errors, throttling, unexpected responses) but lack server-side visibility.
rootCause: Graph API calls can fail for various reasons (permission, throttling, backend errors) that are not fully surfaced in client-side error responses. Server-side correlation is needed to identify root cause.
solution: Use Jarvis/Geneva dashboard https://portal.microsoftgeneva.com/s/DA2B3307 to trace MS Graph API calls. Filter logs using correlationId that matches the request-id from the API call request/response header (Where correlationId == <request-id in header>). This provides server-side visibility into Graph API processing.

symptom: Cloud-only user cannot log on to VM joined to AAD Domain Services (AADDS) managed domain. Authentication fails after enabling AADDS.
rootCause: Cloud-only users need NTLM/Kerberos password hash synced to AADDS. Hash generation is only triggered after a password change/reset post-AADDS enablement.
solution: Have cloud-only users change or reset their password after AADDS is enabled to trigger NTLM/Kerberos hash generation. Wait ~20 minutes for sync to complete. For synced users from on-premises AD, ensure password hash sync is enabled in AAD Connect.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Readiness path/Readiness by topics/AAD Domain Service.md

symptom: LDAPS connection to AAD Domain Services managed domain fails. Clients cannot establish secure LDAP connection on port 636.
rootCause: Certificate used for LDAPS does not meet requirements: subject name must match the managed domain name (wildcard *.domain format), or certificate is expired/not properly uploaded as PFX.
solution: Verify certificate subject matches *.aadds-managed-domain-name. Ensure certificate is valid, has the correct key usage (server auth EKU), and is uploaded as PFX format. Use ldp.exe to test LDAPS connectivity. Check NSG rules allow port 636 inbound.

symptom: Customer needs to enable Seamless SSO (Desktop SSO / DSSO) in Mooncake but it is not available through AAD Connect wizard GUI. Feature is in public preview and not officially announced.
rootCause: Seamless SSO in Mooncake is a partial feature (public preview). The AAD Connect wizard does not expose the DSSO toggle for Mooncake tenants, so it must be enabled via PowerShell.
solution: On AAD Connect server: 1) Open PowerShell, navigate to programfiles Microsoft Azure Active Directory Connect. 2) Import-Module AzureADSSO.psd1. 3) New-AzureADSSOAuthenticationContext (enter global admin creds). 4) Get-AzureADSSOStatus to verify disabled. 5) Enable-AzureADSSOForest (enter domain admin creds as DOMAINNAME username format). 6) Enable-AzureADSSO -Enable true. 7) Get-AzureADSSOStatus to verify enabled. Note: domain listed in output should be local domain name. Mooncake DSSO network endpoint: https://autologon.hybridauth.microsoft.cn. PG contact: bpolatoglu@microsoft.com.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Sync/AAD Connect/Feature _ How to/Seamless SSO in Mooncake.md
